pending_context.notes: "I'm with videographer Petna Dalico"; cross-check with other transcripts where the videographer name appears // [PASS-6: rejected — speculation without corroboration
"Down floor to shore, back in the area near... It's a place called Dubgaate, back in that area. Of Old Dawson Road"; possibly "Tubercultate" or "Dougherty" or local farming community name
"To Sassar, which is in the city of Limson, Sassar"; possibly a Sasser sub-community name
"One room school. The name of it was Panty Grove"; a one-room Terrell County school in the 1940s — local name not in the canonical corpus
"My first teacher's name was Vanny Wakefield, I believe"; speaker's own recollection
"It was the Zine Hill, a Sardis Baptist church"; "Zion Hill" is a canonical Black-Baptist church name throughout the rural South; Sardis Baptist also common
"Some of the people's ADL sales, St. Robert, it's Catherine Mallory"; "ADL" likely a person's initials, not the Anti-Defamation League
Same passage as #65.31
Speaker's recollection of adult literacy class students
"Gladys Clark, who was not deceased and his sister, Anadine Clark"; speaker's local civil rights cohort
Documented historical figure; the speaker's claim that he was murdered in 1994 should be cross-checked but is plausible
"Working in the CNA, Citiburn nursing system"; the speaker was a Certified Nursing Assistant (CNA); "Citiburn" likely a mistranscription of a unit name
"Here, come to Freedom Riders" — local white folks lumping all civil rights workers under the "Freedom Rider" label; speaker is reporting the local epithet, not literal Freedom Rider identity

pending_context.whisper: a young lady named Buffa Global
pending_context.notes: Bertha Gober was one of two Albany State students expelled for participating in the November 1961 train-station sit-in; the McCullar fall-1961 march matches

"they would talk in like Chasarai, Don Steward, Pratia"; Don Harris is the canonical SNCC SW Georgia field worker (Albany / Americus jail), recurring across the SW Georgia interviews — Pass 1 noted uncertainty but the SW Georgia field-worker triad of Sherrod/Hall/Allen + Don Harris is canonical
"We would sickle this mess. They would say, I black people are doing fine"; speaker disfluency, Whisper rendering uncertain — possibly "we'd hear all this"

Pass 1 noted speaker-originating uncertainty; cross-corpus the David Cline crew videographer's name is rendered inconsistently — the canonical spelling needs cross-reference with other Cline-team transcripts (e.g., Howell #67, where Cline is also interviewer) // [PASS-6: rejected — speculation without corroboration

"Working in the CNA, Citiburn nursing system"; "Citi Burn" is likely a Whisper rendering of CCU (Critical Care Unit) — McCullar was a CNA in the hospital's intensive-care division at Phoebe Putney; Pass 1 had medium uncertainty on Surgical Burn Unit

pending_context.whisper: I tried to buy it, always working
pending_context.candidate_correction: I'd try to remember, always working
pending_context.notes: "I tried to buy it, always working. She was always looking for something to do"; speaker disfluency about her mother — "I tried to buy it" is a Whisper artifact, likely "Let me see / I'd tell you / I'd describe"

pending_context.whisper: And he was Santa Claus at the post office and washes
pending_context.candidate_correction: and he was the Santa Claus at the post office Christmas
pending_context.notes: "And the registrar would leave the office. And he was Santa Claus at the post office and washes"; the speaker is describing the Terrell County voter registrar's day job — likely "and he was [working at] the post office and..." — Whisper artifact

"She didn't care her counting. All I need her to do is to do what I ask her to do. And I pay." The Albany employer is refusing to fire McCullar's mother; "didn't care her counting" appears to be Whisper artifact, likely "didn't care nothing about it" or "didn't care, hired her" — speaker disfluency. Best reading: the Albany employer didn't care about McCullar's mother's voter-registration history.

pending_context.whisper: the police would press the music
pending_context.candidate_correction: the police would press [stop] the music
pending_context.notes: "the police would press the music, the sheriff, and his devotees would come"; "press the music" is a Whisper artifact — likely "stop the music" or "press [against] the music" (interrupt the singing). Pass 2 P2.10 confirms the singing context. Adversarial review may resolve.

"I'm trying to remember the first one. I mean, I was a linguist, Charles Sarrard. I believe in my mistake." Speaker disfluency: "I was a linguist" is Whisper artifact for "if I'm not mistaken" (compare Pass 2 P2.19 same speaker's "I am from Not Mistake" → "If I am not mistaken"). Speaker is recalling her first SNCC encounter, hedging on certainty.

"But it seemed like when it came to the food, I guess they're just like the dottation took out all of that." Speaker context (Albany State dining-hall food was bad despite student dues paid); "dottation" is a Whisper artifact — likely "donation" (charitable food contribution that got siphoned) or "deduction" (room-and-board fee). Adversarial review may resolve.

"things had started to enter accounting. It's sort of not open labor." Pass 2 P2.5 caught the second half ("not open labor" → "out in the open"); Pass 4 catches the first half: "enter accounting" is a Whisper artifact for "integrate" (or "enter, you know..." speaker self-interruption). Speaker context is the political climate softening in fall 1962.

"Like on Wednesday night, while chair of county was on Wednesday night, we would go to the county if there was on Tuesday night, back in football." Speaker is describing the rotating SW Georgia mass-meeting schedule across counties; "back in football" is a Whisper artifact — possibly "back in [Lee County] / back in fall / back and forth." Speaker disfluency. Adversarial review may resolve.

Same passage. "while chair of county" appears to be Whisper artifact for one of the SW Georgia counties in the SNCC voter-registration triad (Terrell, Lee, Worth, Dougherty). Adversarial models with SW Georgia gazetteer may resolve.

"By this time, they had started the foundation to reveal my dollar. We would put chairs there." Speaker context: the congregation began rebuilding Mt. Olive Baptist Church after the August 1962 burning. "Reveal my dollar" is a Whisper artifact — likely "rebuild Mt. Olive" or "rebuild — Mr. [name]" or "rebuild the altar." The foundation-rebuilding context is unambiguous; the exact phrase is not.

"Because we registered voters. Now, five here. Did that happen because she had actually successfully registered?" Whisper artifact — interviewer interjection mangled by Whisper. Best reading: "Wait, hear / I see" or "Now I hear." Low confidence, speaker-disfluency-class.

"So after the tragedy at Manawa, what is the movement do then?" Speaker context: tragedy = Mt. Olive Baptist Church burning August 1962. "Manawa" is a Whisper artifact for "Mt. Olive" — possibly a phonetic collapse of "Mt. Olive" → "Mt. Oliva" → "Manawa." Adversarial review warranted but the referent is unambiguous.

pending_context.whisper: she's also where she did it
pending_context.candidate_correction: she's also aware [that] she did it
pending_context.notes: "And she's also where she did it. And this is a white woman." Whisper artifact — likely "she was aware [that] she did it" or "she was over where she did it." Speaker context: the head cook at the school where McCullar's mother worked was the white woman commenting on McCullar's march participation.
Alternate reading for 65.P4.16 — the mass-meeting county-rotation context fits "back and forth" between counties on different nights.

"To make sure nobody suspicious cars, were you looking for vehicles? Anyone trying to set a fire? Are any strange cars pulling in the yard" — the interviewer's question is mangled by Whisper into "Are any strange cars" instead of "If any strange cars" or "and any strange cars." Common-noun grammar fix.

pending_context.whisper: sit down on the lower level
pending_context.notes: "You could go to a movie and then you had to sit in the back and sit down on the lower level." Speaker is describing pre-VRA Jim Crow movie theater seating — Black patrons sat in the BALCONY (upper level), not the lower level, in canonical Jim Crow theater segregation. Whisper or speaker reversal of "lower/upper." Likely "no longer sit in the back and sit down on the lower level" — speaker is comparing post-VRA freedom to pre-VRA constraints. Adversarial review for the exact phrasing.

coverage_flag_legend.P-NN: Partial read covered NN% of bytes

pass9_metadata.credit_formula: match_factor * min(0.10, max(0, 0.02 + 0.001*healed - 0.005*fails)); credit = 0 if healed=0 or match<0.3
